name: Pickled Mixed Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 2 cup Peeled and cut carrots (1/2-inch pieces)
2 cup Cut sweet red peppers (1/2-inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 10 pints
Procedure:
1. Wash cucumbers and cut into 1-inch slices, removing 1/16 inch from the blossom end and discarding.
2. Combine vegetables in a container, cover with 2 inches of cubed or crushed ice, and refrigerate for 3 to 4 hours.
3. In an 8-quart kettle, combine vinegar, mustard, salt, sugar, celery seed, mustard seed, cloves, and turmeric. Mix well.
4. Bring mixture to a boil.
5. Drain vegetables and add them to the hot pickling solution in the kettle. Cover and bring to a boil slowly.
6. Drain vegetables but save the pickling solution.
7. Fill sterile pint jars or clean quart jars with vegetables, leaving 1/2-inch headspace. Add pickling solution, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
Processing:
Refer to Table 1 for recommended processing times in a boiling-water canner based on altitude.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Pickled Mixed Vegetables in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 min
Altitude above 1,000 ft: Add 2 minutes per 1,000 ft to the processing time
Jar Size: Quarts
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 min
Altitude above 1,000 ft: Add 3 minutes per 1,000 ft to the processing time
